behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Real failures are often at module boundaries: CSV parser line indexing, schema validator output, and DLQ writer payload mapping. A pure unit test of validator logic can miss off-by-one row numbers or dropped metadata during wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run the row-processing path end-to-end in-process with a fixture CSV (including header + invalid rows), real schema validation module, and a test DLQ sink (local queue/table). Assert invalid rows are routed to DLQ and each record contains the original CSV row number.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic transformation logic in one function/object boundary. Failures are reproducible by direct inputs and expected outputs; no I/O or runtime infrastructure is required.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test for the date-normalization function with representative ISO 8601, US (MM/DD/YYYY), and European (DD/MM/YYYY) inputs, plus edge cases (timezone suffixes, invalid dates). Assert exact canonical output or explicit validation error.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: This depends on process memory behavior, runtime throughput, and timeout handling under realistic load. In-process mocks can hide buffering, GC pressure, and scheduler/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process against a large synthetic CSV (500k rows), with production-like timeout and memory limits. Measure wall-clock completion and peak RSS; fail if timeout is exceeded or memory crosses threshold/causes OOM.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Retry/backoff correctness often depends on real HTTP/client behavior (status handling, retry hooks, timing). Pure mocks can falsely pass while real transport or SDK integration fails.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real HTTP S3-compatible test endpoint (or local server) scripted to return 503 for initial requests then 200. Run the pipeline fetch step and assert successful completion plus retry timing pattern consistent with exponential backoff.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: This failure boundary includes durable DB state plus process restart semantics (checkpoint/offset/idempotency). Mocked DB or no real restart can miss duplicate-write races and transaction edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against a real PostgreSQL instance with a controlled mid-batch interruption (terminate process after partial commit), then restart from checkpoint. Assert final table contains exactly one row per source record (no duplicates) and batch completes.
